toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run these tests and collect a crash dump if the test host crashes. In .NET, you can use the `--crashdump` option (or `--blame-crash` depending on the test platform) to collect crash dumps.

Since this project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true`, it's using the newer Microsoft Testing Platform integration. For the newer testing platform, the crash dump collection is done via `--crashdump` option.

For the older VSTest-based approach, you'd use `--blame-crash`.

Since `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is true, this uses the Microsoft Testing Platform. The command would be:


Running the test with the crashdump option to capture any crash diagnostics.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.content: The `--crashdump` flag needs to be passed through to the test runner via `--`. Let me try the correct syntax:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` flag isn't being recognized properly. This is because of the `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` setting. With the newer Microsoft Testing Platform integration via `dotnet test`, the options need to be passed differently.

Let me check the correct syntax. With the Microsoft Testing Platform integration, the options should be passed after `--` separator, like:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

Or we might need to use the `--blame-crash` flag which is the VSTest-based approach.

Actually, looking more carefully at the error, with `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`, the `dotnet test` command should support the testing platform options. 

The real problem is that `--crashdump` is being passed to MSBuild instead of reaching the test runner. I need to use the `--` separator to tell `dotnet test` to forward the remaining arguments directly to the test runner rather than processing them as MSBuild options.

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. The correct command is:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

The `--` separator passes `--crashdump` through to the Microsoft Testing Platform runner (enabled by `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` in your `.csproj`). If a test crashes the host process, a crash dump (`.dmp` file) will be written to the test results directory.
